Agree with the current assessment that option A (Proximal Renal Tubular Acidosis) correctly represents the exception here. The key point remains consistent—PRSA results in hyperchloremic metabolic alkalosis rather than a normal anion gap metabolic acidosis. Adding another perspective, PRSA affects the reabsorption mechanism specifically targeting HCO₃⁻, leading to increased serum Cl⁻ levels which oppose the development of a true anion-gap metabolic acidosis where there would be expected increases in unmeasured anions.
